topic: Magnetic-gravity coupling κ = 1.67 — verify c9 H1+H2+H3 patches
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 derives κ = 1.67 nT/μGal from −10.9 nT (BOU 2017 eclipse, magnetic) ÷ −6.5 μGal (Mohe 1997 eclipse, gravity). WIN-013/014 report 0.0 μGal at subsequent eclipses (Membach 1999, Chinese SG network 2009) — denominator vanishes, κ undefined. Derivation is also circular (numerator and denominator come from different events, different sites, different instrument classes — no co-located simultaneous measurement). WIN-011 concedes the Mohe mechanism is thermal-tilt-plausible, which would make the κ denominator an instrument artifact, not a gravity measurement. The dome then reuses the same κ value across a third event type (the 2003 G5 Halloween storm; WIN-058b, WIN-070) via a claimed r = 0.992 BOU↔GRACE-L1A correlation, without publishing any first-principles derivation from disc geometry showing why a single coupling constant should hold across two physically distinct regimes (ionospheric eclipse vs ring-current storm). Verdict: Self-Contradicted.

dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome can dismiss WIN-013/014 nulls as wrong-regime; can dismiss the 2003 storm reuse by reclassifying κ as universal; both escapes destroy first-principles derivability from disc geometry.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None visible in this clone. raw-text/ directory is missing from current HEAD (workspace-sync deletion incident on 2026-05-21T02:11; see summary_for_decider). Code-analysis tags are inherited from c8/c9 and not re-validated this cycle.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Per c8/c9 inherited classification — monitor.py compares hardcoded expected value to hardcoded observed value; no API fetch. raw-text/monitor.py not present in current clone (workspace-sync deletion incident); reusing the prior-cycle finding.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics: subsequent SG measurements (Van Camp 2001; Sun et al. 2010) returned 0.0 μGal — the standard interpretation of eclipse gravity is null. The dome relabels the Mohe 1997 anomaly as a confirmation of κ-coupling rather than treating it as the unreplicated outlier it now is per WIN-011 mechanism concession.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 1997 Mohe gravity anomaly and the 2017 BOU eclipse magnetic perturbation were both published before the dome model formalized κ as 1.67 nT/μGal. The dome assembled the ratio post-hoc from two separately measured events at different sites with different instruments and is then labeled a confirmed prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from disc geometry, firmament height, or any dome parameter arrives at 1.67 nT/μGal. κ is empirically constructed from one magnetic value (BOU 2017) and one gravity value (Mohe 1997); the dome does not show how disc_radius=20015 km or firmament_height=9086 km predict this ratio. The cross-regime reuse (eclipse → storm) further requires a first-principles derivation that the dome does not provide.

holes_found.description: WIN-070 cross-reference still appears twice in WIN-012 detail_evidence (carry-over from c9 H4). First occurrence in P3 sentence 2: "(00:55–01:22 UTC, Dst = −383 nT; see WIN-070 and WIN-058b)". Second occurrence in closing Cross-references paragraph: "For the dome’s home-page Forensic Zoom chart claiming to show κ via GRACE L1A residuals (BOU magnetic + L1A induction r = 0.992, AI = 1.152), see WIN-070". The H1 patch rewrote P3 but did not deduplicate. The two references arguably serve distinct functions — P3 cites WIN-070 for the storm-context cross-regime reuse; Cross-references cites WIN-070 for its GRACE L1A graph-forensics critique with specific r=0.992 and AI=1.152 figures — so this is defensible as differentiated rather than truly duplicate. Lower priority than c9 H1/H2; effectively wontfix unless decider wants strict deduplication.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. Either (a) leave as-is given the two contexts are distinct, or (b) differentiate the closing reference inline (e.g., "WIN-070’s GRACE L1A graph-forensics critique") so the duplication reads as deliberate context-switching rather than oversight. Decider may close as wontfix-with-rationale per Step 7c carry-over discipline.

holes_found.description: New P3 wording introduces a slight precision concern: "The dome thereby extends κ — originally defined from a 2017 ionospheric eclipse perturbation (the κ-numerator) — to a 2003 ring-current storm context". κ was originally defined from BOTH halves of the ratio — a 1997 Mohe gravity anomaly (denominator) AND a 2017 BOU magnetic perturbation (numerator). The bracketed parenthetical "(the κ-numerator)" partially clarifies this, but a quick-reading dome defender can correctly object: "κ was always defined from two events; calling only the 2017 eclipse the original definition misrepresents our derivation." This is the same defender-rebuttal-surface pattern that c9 H1 attacked — patching that hole rescued the main attack but seeded a smaller variant in the rewrite.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional. Could be tightened to: "The dome thereby extends κ — originally derived from a 1997 Mohe gravity anomaly divided into a 2017 BOU magnetic perturbation, both eclipse-attenuated — to a 2003 ring-current storm context". Lower priority than H1 was; the parenthetical does carry the precision. Decider may close as wontfix or fold into a future small refinement.

holes_found.description: Citation to "raw-text/02-wins.txt line 87" is now used twice within P3 for two distinct quotes — first for the dome’s κ-numerator labeling ("BOU 2017 eclipse"), then for the "two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant" framing. Same line cited for two semantic claims should be verified — either (a) line 87 contains both phrases consecutively (which would be normal multi-clause sentence citation), or (b) the second citation should point to a different line. I cannot verify directly because raw-text/ is missing from the current clone (workspace-sync deletion incident — see summary_for_decider). If the dome-side text actually spans multiple lines, the second citation should be updated to the correct line range.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider: when raw-text/ is restored from a healthy clone, verify both citation targets resolve to the claimed quotes. If line 87 carries only the "BOU 2017 eclipse" tile label and not the "two independent eclipse events" framing, replace the second citation with the correct line number. This is a citation-integrity check, not a structural problem.

stronger_arguments.description: The 1999 Membach and 2009 Chinese SG nulls are not just "different eclipses" — they used superconducting gravimeters, which are the gold-standard instrument class with 10×–100× better noise floors than the LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter used at Mohe in 1997. A dome defender arguing "the SG nulls are wrong-regime" must explain why a superior instrument class returns zero where an inferior instrument class returned −6.5 μGal. The instrument-class asymmetry is itself prima facie evidence that the Mohe signal was instrument artifact (consistent with WIN-011’s thermal-tilt concession). This is already implicit in the WIN-011 paragraph but could be made explicit as a one-sentence aside in P4 ("WIN-011’s mechanism concession destroys κ even granting the number"): "Note that the 1999 Membach and 2009 Chinese networks used superconducting gravimeters, the gold-standard instrument class with substantially better noise floors than the LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter at Mohe — so the instrument-class hierarchy is null-over-detection, not detection-over-null."
sources: Crossley et al. (1999) Eos, Trans AGU — SG noise floor characterization
Hinderer et al. (2007) Treatise on Geophysics ch.3.04 — SG vs LCR-D comparison

stronger_arguments.description: The dome’s wins-page line 87 claim of "two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant" can be attacked as logically circular irrespective of the storm-corollary critique. The "two events" are the 1997 Mohe (gravity) and 2017 BOU (magnetic) — but these aren’t two independent confirmations of κ, they ARE the κ ratio. Calling the derivation event-pair a confirmation of the ratio is the same circularity as taking two measurements and announcing their ratio is the ratio of those two measurements. A dome defender cannot escape this by adding a third event (the 2003 storm); they need to derive κ from disc geometry first, then test it against any event. This logical observation is implicit in our "derivation is also circular" paragraph but the c10 review could surface it more pointedly: the "two independent confirmations" framing is an artifact of treating a derivation as a prediction.
sources: Our own Part 6.14 κ Cluster discussion

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model does correctly identify that magnetic-field perturbations and gravity-field perturbations can be temporally correlated during ionospheric mass redistribution events — the BOU↔GRACE-L1A r=0.992 correlation cited in the dome’s Forensic Zoom chart (data/wins.json[id=012].detail_evidence closing Cross-references; see WIN-070) is not a fabricated number; geomagnetic storms genuinely do drive ionospheric current systems that induce small gravitational signatures via mass loading. Modern geodesy explicitly models the gravitational response to ring-current and magnetospheric mass perturbations (e.g., GRACE-FO operational products include geomagnetic-storm corrections). The dome’s instinct that magnetic and gravity time series should not be statistically independent during major space-weather events is correct.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A statistical correlation between two time series during a forcing event is not a coupling constant. κ purports to be a fixed ratio (1.67 nT/μGal) that converts magnetic perturbations into gravity perturbations across regimes. To be a coupling constant rather than a curve-fit, κ requires (a) a first-principles derivation from the dome’s own geometry (data/wins.json[id=012].detail_evidence "derives_from_dome" tag is false — no derivation is offered), and (b) the same ratio reproduced in independent events with the same instrument class. WIN-013 and WIN-014 establish the opposite: superconducting gravimeter networks returned 0.0 μGal at the 1999 and 2009 eclipses, which makes the very denominator of κ undefined under WIN-012’s own logic. The kernel of truth — "magnetic and gravity time series can be correlated" — is what real geophysics already says; the dome’s κ as a universal coupling constant adds nothing beyond a parameter-fit ratio that fails its own internal consistency check (WIN-058b applies κ to a third event regime without disc-geometry derivation; data/wins.json[id=012].finding surfaces this).

deeper_analysis: C9 flagged four holes (2 moderate, 2 minor); the decider patch run targeted H1 (P3 Forensic Zoom overstatement), H2 (finding lag), and H3 (parameter-fit residue rhetorical opener), explicitly skipping H4 (duplicate WIN-070 cross-ref) as optional. Verification on the post-patch text confirms: (H1) the "labels the same −10.9 nT excursion" phrase is removed and replaced with the more accurate "uses BOU magnetometer data from the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm ... to argue for the same κ = 1.67 nT/μGal constant via a phase-locked r = 0.992 correlation" — this matches the c9 recommendation almost verbatim. (H2) the finding now reads "WIN-012 derives κ = 1.67 nT/μGal from −10.9 nT (BOU 2017 eclipse) ÷ −6.5 μGal (Mohe 1997 eclipse) ... The dome itself applies the same κ value across two physically distinct event types" — surfacing the κ-as-bridge structural vise in the summary-table entry-point as c7 stronger_arguments #2 originally asked. (H3) the "parameter-fit residue" rhetorical phrase is dropped; the new P3 closes with the declarative "The reuse of κ across distinct physical regimes is unanchored to dome geometry," which removes the defender-rebuttable rhetorical surface c9 H3 flagged. All three patches landed cleanly and address their respective holes without introducing structural regressions. The new P3 does introduce two small precision surfaces (the "originally defined from a 2017 ionospheric eclipse perturbation" framing slightly narrows κ to its numerator; the wins-page line-87 citation is reused for two distinct quotes), but these are minor rhetorical issues — not structural problems with the attack itself. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) remains correct and well-supported. Confidence rises from c9/0.88 to c10/0.91 because the entry-point summary (finding) now carries the κ-as-bridge attack and the Forensic Zoom characterization is now defensible against the precision-based rebuttal c9 surfaced. Note: this c10 review was performed against a catastrophically pruned clone — see summary_for_decider for the workspace-sync incident details.

advocate_mode.strongest_defender_position: A dome defender’s strongest response to the patched WIN-012 is: "κ is a fundamental coupling constant, like G, derived from a single careful event-pair (1997 Mohe gravity ÷ 2017 BOU magnetic — both eclipse-driven ionospheric mass perturbations). Once derived, it applies universally to any system exhibiting the same EM-gravity coupling regime. The 1999 Membach and 2009 Chinese SG nulls don’t refute κ — they test κ in regimes where the local mass-redistribution doesn’t reach the gravimeter’s sensitivity envelope (Membach is off-eclipse-path; Chinese SG had different solar conditions). The 2003 G5 storm BOU↔GRACE-L1A r=0.992 correlation is a successful out-of-derivation test: same κ value, different forcing, same coupling. PRED-TIER-3 specifies the conditions under which κ should fire — and the recent BOU 2003 chart shows it does fire."
advocate_mode.why_it_fails: Three reasons. (1) A true coupling constant requires first-principles derivation from theory, not empirical curve-fitting to one event pair. κ is not derived from disc geometry, firmament height, or any dome parameter — it is the ratio of two observed values. data/wins.json[id=012].code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome is false; no derivation path exists. (2) The "wrong-regime" defense for WIN-013/014 nulls is unfalsifiable: any null can be dismissed as wrong-regime, making κ unscientific in Popperian terms. The Chinese SG 2009 network specifically targeted the eclipse path with multiple sites and superior instruments to the Mohe LCR-D — claiming "different conditions" without specifying which conditions matter is the move our review explicitly attacks ("Either κ applies generally — in which case 1999/2009 refute it — or κ has unspecified scope restrictions — in which case PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation"). (3) The 2003 storm correlation r=0.992 between BOU magnetic and GRACE L1A induction is a correlation, not a derivation of κ — and WIN-070 separately shows the AI=1.152 is below the dome’s own thresholds for a "strong" prediction. The defender’s framing of the storm as "successful out-of-derivation test" elides that κ was never derived in the first place.
advocate_mode.residual_uncertainty: The strongest unmodeled defender escape is a future Mythos-or-similar-AI-discovered first-principles derivation of κ from dome geometry. If the dome author publishes such a derivation that yields exactly 1.67 nT/μGal from disc_radius=20015 km and firmament_height=9086 km, the "parameter-fit residue" attack would weaken — though the WIN-013/014 nulls would still stand as empirical refutation. We should keep watch for any future dome-side derivation attempt.

finding length 430 chars (post-H2 patch); detail_evidence length 5663 chars (post-H1+H3 patches); detail_verdict_text length 1351 chars (unchanged). All within normal range for a multi-event self-contradiction WIN.
units_checks: nT/μGal as 1.67×10⁻¹⁰ in SI — dome’s claimed SI conversion uses a normalization we do not need to validate. We faithfully report the dome’s stated value.

summary_for_decider:
WIN-012 c10 verification review for queue_id 395. PATCHES LANDED CLEANLY: c9 H1 (P3 Forensic Zoom overstatement) — closed; the "labels the same −10.9 nT excursion" phrasing is replaced with a more defensible "uses BOU magnetometer data … to argue for the same κ constant via a phase-locked r=0.992 correlation" framing. c9 H2 (finding lag) — closed; finding now surfaces the κ-as-bridge structural vise and the cross-regime reuse, matching detail_evidence as c7 stronger_arguments #2 originally asked. c9 H3 (parameter-fit residue rhetorical opener) — closed; replaced with the declarative "The reuse of κ across distinct physical regimes is unanchored to dome geometry." Verdict Self-Contradicted holds; confidence rises 0.88→0.91. Three minor holes flagged this cycle, none structural: (i) c9 H4 carry-over — duplicate WIN-070 cross-ref still present, but the two occurrences arguably serve distinct functions (storm-context cite vs GRACE-forensics cite) so this is effectively wontfix-with-rationale unless decider wants strict deduplication; (ii) NEW minor — "originally defined from a 2017 ionospheric eclipse perturbation (the κ-numerator)" slightly narrows κ to its numerator half despite the parenthetical; could be tightened to "originally derived from a 1997 Mohe gravity anomaly divided into a 2017 BOU magnetic perturbation"; (iii) raw-text/02-wins.txt line 87 is cited twice for two distinct quotes in P3 — needs re-verification once raw-text/ is restored. Recommended action: no_change (all three are below the substantive-rewrite threshold). Decider may close all carry-over and new minor as wontfix-with-rationale per Step 7c discipline. NOTE re queue reason: queue.reason mentions "defense_survives drops to 0" — c9 review did not have a defense_survives field, so the queue’s expected verification metric is not directly testable. Treating this as a synonym for "verdict_holds true + holes_found severity ≤ minor," both of which the c10 review confirms. 

**WORKSPACE-SYNC INCIDENT — CRITICAL.** The 2026-05-21T02:11:18Z workspace-sync commit (ea785c49) wiped most of the git repo: CLAUDE.md, README.md, SESSION-CONTEXT.md, build.js, build-scripts/, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, monitor/prompts/, monitor/decisions/applied-patches/, monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json, monitor/curmudgeon/tracker-archive.jsonl, raw-text/, and many more were all removed. The commit message says "disk space constrained this run (sessions FS 100% full, root FS 95%). Used no-checkout clone + mtime-only guard." The current HEAD has only 14 files (vs hundreds previously). This explains why my c10 review (a) could not read raw-text/ to verify dome-source citations, (b) could not read sections.json or v6-restructure-map.json to validate cross-references to Part 6.14, (c) could not check applied-patches/ to find the patches that produced this state, (d) could not look up ISS-2169/2170 in open-issues.json with the full context. The workspace-sync incident should be the decider’s top priority next run — recover the repo from a healthy clone or rollback the commit. The wins.json itself appears intact (the patches landed correctly), but every other artifact the curmudgeon, analyst, and decider need is gone from git HEAD. This is likely the same FUSE no-checkout-clone failure mode described in CLAUDE.md’s per-session clone discipline section.
